description: Therapeutic targets for genetic diseases - oncogenes, tumor suppressors, and monogenic disease genes. V2: length-matched distractors to eliminate length bias.

facts.context: TP53 (p53) is mutated in over 50% of cancers. Its normal function is:
facts.truth: tumor suppressor that activates cell cycle arrest and apoptosis in response to DNA damage
distractors: oncogene that drives cell proliferation and blocks differentiation during normal tissue growth
structural protein that maintains nuclear envelope integrity without any signaling activity
growth factor receptor embedded in the cell surface that amplifies mitogenic signals

facts.context: BRCA1/BRCA2 mutations increase cancer risk because these genes normally:
facts.truth: mediate homologous recombination repair of double-strand DNA breaks
distractors: activate receptor tyrosine kinase proliferation pathways in epithelial tissue
suppress adaptive immune surveillance of incipient tumors in breast tissue
regulate mitochondrial oxidative metabolism exclusively without nuclear roles

facts.context: KRAS G12C mutations are oncogenic because the mutant protein:
facts.truth: is locked in GTP-bound active state, constitutively signaling proliferation
distractors: cannot bind any nucleotide and remains permanently inert in the cytoplasm
has greatly enhanced GTPase activity causing rapid cycling between conformations
acts as a potent tumor suppressor when mutated in the G12 codon position

facts.context: The BCR-ABL fusion in chronic myeloid leukemia creates:
facts.truth: constitutively active tyrosine kinase driving proliferation (target of imatinib)
distractors: catalytically inactive kinase that requires upstream phosphorylation for activation
potent tumor suppressor protein that normally restrains myeloid cell expansion
cell surface receptor that binds circulating growth factors to trigger division

facts.truth: transcriptionally activating genes for proliferation, metabolism, and ribosome biogenesis
distractors: globally suppressing all RNA polymerase II transcription across the entire genome
inducing permanent cell cycle arrest through upregulation of CDK inhibitor proteins
promoting DNA double-strand break repair exclusively without affecting cell growth

facts.context: Duchenne muscular dystrophy is caused by mutations in DMD gene. The protein dystrophin:
facts.truth: links cytoskeleton to extracellular matrix, stabilizing muscle fiber membranes
distractors: is a secreted hormone that circulates in the blood and regulates muscle metabolism
functions only in motor neurons where it controls neurotransmitter release at synapses
is a nuclear transcription factor that activates sarcomeric gene expression programs

facts.context: Cystic fibrosis is caused by CFTR mutations. CFTR protein normally:
facts.truth: acts as chloride channel regulating ion and water transport in epithelia
distractors: is a structural component of the ciliary axoneme required for mucociliary clearance
degrades accumulated mucus in the airways through its intrinsic protease activity
transports glucose and other simple sugars across epithelial cell membranes actively

facts.context: Huntington's disease is caused by CAG repeat expansion in HTT gene. The pathogenic mechanism is:
facts.truth: toxic gain-of-function from polyglutamine aggregates in neurons
distractors: complete loss of normal huntingtin function without any toxic protein species
direct RNA toxicity from expanded CUG repeats without protein translation involved
accumulation of somatic mitochondrial DNA mutations triggered by repeat instability

facts.context: Spinal muscular atrophy is caused by SMN1 loss. SMN2 differs by:
facts.truth: C-to-T change causing exon 7 skipping, producing mostly non-functional protein
distractors: complete deletion of the coding sequence resulting in no transcript being produced
producing an identical and fully functional protein at the same expression levels
a frameshift mutation in exon 1 that truncates the protein after ten amino acids

facts.context: Sickle cell disease is caused by HBB E6V mutation. The therapeutic approach using gene editing:
facts.truth: reactivates fetal hemoglobin (HBF) by disrupting BCL11A erythroid enhancer
distractors: directly corrects the E6V point mutation back to wild-type glutamic acid codon
deletes the entire HBB locus including upstream regulatory elements from the genome
inserts a fully synthetic hemoglobin gene cassette at a genomic safe harbor locus

facts.context: EGFR-mutant lung cancers respond to tyrosine kinase inhibitors because:
facts.truth: tumors are oncogene-addicted and depend on mutant EGFR signaling for survival
distractors: EGFR mutations independently sensitize cancer cells to standard cytotoxic chemotherapy
tyrosine kinase inhibitors directly induce apoptosis regardless of EGFR mutation status
wild-type EGFR is inherently more sensitive to small molecule kinase inhibition than mutant

facts.truth: inhibits E2F transcription factors, blocking G1 to S phase transition
distractors: promotes cell cycle progression by phosphorylating cyclin-dependent kinase substrates
functions exclusively in retinal progenitor cells without roles in other tissue types
activates the intrinsic apoptosis pathway directly by permeabilizing mitochondrial membranes
